description_long: This object is a small bird with a streamlined body, predominantly slate-gray on the upperparts and wings, and a warm orange-brown coloration on the underparts and face. The bird has a long, pointed beak that is straight and well-suited for probing or pecking. Its wings are compact and close to the body, with subtle white markings visible along the flanks and wing edges. The tail is short and appears squared off, blending seamlessly with the body. The legs are not clearly visible, but the overall form suggests a perching bird commonly found in urban parks or wooded areas.
description: A small, slate-gray and orange-brown bird with a pointed beak and short, squared tail. Subtle white markings accent its wings and flanks.
description_view_0: Side view showing the bird's streamlined body, pointed beak, and orange-brown underparts.
description_view_1: Rear view highlighting the short, squared tail and the uniform gray coloration of the back and wings.
description_view_2: Opposite side view displaying the bird's profile, beak, and the transition from gray upperparts to orange-brown underparts.
description_view_3: Front view emphasizing the pointed beak, rounded head, and the symmetry of the bird's body.
attribute_plumage_pattern: Solid gray upperparts with orange-brown underparts and subtle white markings on the wings and flanks.
